title: Hieracium cremnaeiforme
taxonomy: Hieracium cremnaeiforme is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is classified as a member of the genus Hieracium, which consists of several species of hawkweeds. Within the genus, Hieracium cremnaeiforme is assigned to the subgenus Hieracium subg. Hieracium. The species is further categorized as a member of the section Hieracium sect. Hieracium. The classification helps to group and identify similar species within the plant kingdom.

side_effects: Hieracium cremnaeiforme, also known as hawkweed, contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are toxic to humans and animals when ingested. Ingesting hawkweed can cause gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The plant's sap can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Additionally, if hawkweed is ingested in large quantities, it may lead to more severe symptoms such as tremors, seizures, and even liver damage. However, it is essential to note that the severity of these side effects can vary depending on the individual and the amount consumed.
intro: Hieracium cremnaeiforme, a species of the dandelion-like flowering plant genus Hieracium, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Hieracium cremnaeiforme has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium cremnaeiforme include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Hieracium cremnaeiforme is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used as a dried extract in capsule or powder form.
